---
title: "Introduction"
---

TODO: 2–3 sentences framing the "hook." What does the raw/unfiltered data seem to say at first glance, and why is that first impression misleading or incomplete? This sets up the tension your scrollytelling piece will resolve.

TODO: 2–3 sentences describing what dataset you're using and what the reader will be walked through (e.g. "this project explores X to uncover Y, moving from static charts to an interactive scroll experience that reveals Z").

## The Dataset

TODO: 1 short paragraph. Where did this data come from, what does it capture, and over what scope/time window?

TODO: List out the 2–4 attributes that matter most to your story, in the same style as below. Group them by theme if it helps (e.g. "Spatial attributes" vs. "Engineered/derived attributes").

**field_name_1:** What it measures and why it matters to the story.

**field_name_2:** What it measures and why it matters to the story.

**field_name_3:** What it measures and why it matters to the story.

## Dataset Information

**Dataset:** TODO — dataset name

**Source:** TODO — organization/provider

**URL:** TODO — link to raw data

**License:** TODO

**Date Accessed:** TODO

**Time Period:** TODO

TODO: List out all attributes in the dataset

| Attribute Name | Attribute Type | Data Semantics | Cardinality |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :---: |
| **TODO** | Quantitative / Categorical (Nominal/Ordinal) | TODO description | TODO |
| **TODO** | Quantitative / Categorical (Nominal/Ordinal) | TODO description | TODO |
| **TODO** | Quantitative / Categorical (Nominal/Ordinal) | TODO description | TODO |


## Impact and Utility

TODO: 1–2 sentences on who benefits from this analysis and why (e.g. planners, analysts, decision-makers in your domain).

To guide the reader through the data, this story addresses the following questions:

### TODO: Research Question 1

TODO: 2–3 sentences previewing what section 1 (chart1) will reveal.

### TODO: Research Question 2

TODO: 2–3 sentences previewing what section 2 (chart2) will reveal.
